human_verification.notes: Reviewed issue #1473 requirements for information, software, and digital media information compliance handling, source-backed obligation mapping, evidence and confidentiality expectations, legal, editorial, privacy, security, engineering, accessibility, copyright, contractual, attestation, and signoff boundaries, scenario completeness, functionality-map completeness, research-summary completeness, and validated packaging consistency.

review_notes: All required abilities across shared, compliance-controls-and-governance, and professional-and-information-services sets are declared and validated.

Scenario coverage includes normal obligation mapping, missing evidence, conflicting policies and sources, legal and formal signoff refusal, client and content workflow handling, confidentiality conflicts, policy-versus-practice conflicts, adjacent-lane routing, low-confidence facts, review or attestation preparation, terminology normalization, and tenant adaptation.
Package remains specification-based and documents a separate promotion path for any future retained-implementation runtime.

specialist_owned_evidence: Outputs are bounded to evidence-backed information compliance obligation mapping, evidence readiness, review preparation, exception escalation, confidentiality handling, client and content workflow handling, and approval-aware delivery rather than legal, editorial, privacy, security engineering, accessibility, copyright, financial, contractual, attestation, or final signoff authority.

Source set includes NIST CSF 2.0, NIST SP 800-53, NIST SP 800-218, NIST SP 800-61, NIST SP 800-128, FTC personal-information guidance, CISA Secure by Design, CSA Cloud Controls Matrix, W3C WCAG 2.2, U.S. Copyright Office Section 512, and eCFR COPPA with explicit refresh and decay posture.

Boundary logic explicitly prevents unsupported control closeout, unauthorized disclosure, unreviewed product or content approval, privacy or copyright legal interpretation, accessibility legal conclusion, security certification, attestation conclusion, unresolved source conflict, and generic treatment of materially different information, software, and digital media workflows.
notes: No blocking unresolved ambiguity remains; non-blocking ambiguity is recorded as tenant, jurisdiction, audience, product, platform, data category, content-rights, client authority, source-hierarchy, confidentiality, approval, legal, editorial, privacy, security, engineering, accessibility, copyright, contractual, attestation, and regulator-facing prerequisites for specialized information compliance handling.

Runtime remains specification-based; retained-implementation promotion requires separate governance approval, usage evidence, retrieval wiring, mutation safeguards, non-regression validation, audit controls, and explicit runtime posture change.
